method_name: Local construction with a global compatibility obstruction
canonical_construction: Encode the failure of local choices to glue as a compatibility datum and identify the exact vanishing condition.
known_failure_modes: local solvability is mistaken for global solvability
the obstruction depends on noncanonical choices
vanishing is as hard as the root theorem
diagnostic_examples: a cover with pairwise-compatible but not globally-compatible choices
source_references: developer-curated method card; add domain-specific reviewed citations before relying on a named theorem
known_failure_examples: examples with a nonzero global obstruction despite every local test passing

method_name: Minimal counterexample reduced by a natural operation
typical_inputs: a natural quotient, deletion, contraction, or degeneration
canonical_construction: Choose a counterexample minimal for a proof-relevant measure and show a natural operation preserves the target hypotheses while reducing that measure.
usual_intermediate_lemmas: reduction preserves failure of the conclusion
minimal objects have a rigid structure
known_failure_modes: the reduction destroys a target hypothesis
minimality uses the wrong measure
the irreducible endpoint is not classified
diagnostic_examples: smallest examples under the proposed measure
boundary cases where the operation stops reducing
known_failure_examples: a reduction that makes the conclusion easier but invalidates an assumption

method_name: Extremal configuration controls a sharp bound
canonical_construction: Choose an extremizer, derive local exchange inequalities, and use their equality cases to identify the global structure.
usual_intermediate_lemmas: existence or compactness of an extremizer
known_failure_modes: the extremum is not attained
local optimality does not imply global structure
the proposed bound has the wrong equality family
known_failure_examples: multiple unrelated local maxima with different global behavior

method_name: Deform to a tractable special fiber and transport back
typical_conclusions: a bound transported from a special case
canonical_construction: Build a controlled family whose special member is computable and prove the target property is preserved in the required direction.
known_failure_modes: the property jumps in the wrong direction
the degeneration leaves the admissible category
the special fiber introduces extra components
known_failure_examples: a special fiber satisfying the target for accidental singular reasons

method_name: Descent or twisting converts a global form problem into compatibility data
typical_conclusions: an obstruction to a base object
canonical_construction: Track how chosen identifications transform under symmetry and express descent as an exact compatibility equation.
known_failure_modes: effectivity is assumed rather than proved
nonabelian compatibility is linearized incorrectly
automorphism groups vary in the family
known_failure_examples: valid cocycle data that is not effective in the chosen category

method_name: Track an invariant under the operations used by the proof
typical_inputs: a family of reductions or compositions
canonical_construction: Evaluate the invariant on discriminating examples, prove its exact transformation law, and use only the direction needed by the root route.
known_failure_modes: the invariant merely renames the theorem
well-definedness holds but the invariant is proof-irrelevant
the transformation law loses too much information
diagnostic_examples: small examples before and after each operation
known_failure_examples: an invariant constant on all discriminating examples

method_name: Induct through a minimal normal or chief factor
typical_inputs: a property interacting with quotients and normal subgroups
typical_conclusions: a reduction to primitive or monolithic groups
a linear action on a chief factor
canonical_construction: Choose a minimal normal subgroup, solve the quotient problem, and isolate the exact lifting or complement obstruction on the chief factor.
usual_intermediate_lemmas: the chief factor is elementary abelian or characteristically simple
the lifted construction controls the kernel
known_failure_modes: the target property is not quotient closed
lifting changes element orders or conjugacy data
the extension obstruction is silently ignored
source_references: developer-curated structural card; use cited group-theory sources for theorem evidence
known_failure_examples: nonsplit extensions where the quotient construction has no compatible lift

method_name: Translate subgroup structure through character restriction and induction
canonical_construction: Decompose a restriction into conjugate constituents, identify the inertia group, and translate degree, kernel, or codegree identities back into subgroup structure.
known_failure_modes: characteristic hypotheses are omitted
constituent transitivity is mistaken for equality
a numerical identity loses fusion information
source_references: developer-curated structural card; exact Clifford-theory citations required before proof use
known_failure_examples: different inertia groups with the same constituent degrees

method_name: Encode lifting and splitting as an extension obstruction
typical_conclusions: an obstruction class or cocycle equation
canonical_construction: Fix the action, express candidate lifts by cocycles, and identify the exact cohomology class whose vanishing gives the required global object.
known_failure_modes: the action is not fixed
nonabelian kernels are linearized
vanishing after restriction is mistaken for global vanishing
source_references: developer-curated structural card; exact cohomology theorem required for proof evidence
known_failure_examples: locally split but globally nonsplit extensions

method_name: Convert generation or covering conditions into fixed-point geometry
canonical_construction: Choose the natural coset action and translate failure of generation into simultaneous fixed points or containment in conjugate stabilizers.
known_failure_modes: the chosen action is not faithful
maximal subgroups of different types are omitted
separate fixed points are confused with a common fixed point
source_references: developer-curated structural card; classification facts require exact citations
known_failure_examples: elements fixing points in different orbits without a common stabilizer

canonical_construction: Define pairs carrying the desired compatibility, count them by each coordinate, and preserve equality-case information rather than only the final inequality.
known_failure_modes: incidences are not counted with multiplicity
averages hide exceptional fibers
the equality case is discarded
known_failure_examples: one heavy fiber invalidating a uniformity assumption

method_name: Prove existence by a discriminating random construction
canonical_construction: Choose the simplest random object, bound the full bad-event union or dependency graph, and translate positive probability into an exact witness.
known_failure_modes: dependencies are ignored
expectation controls the wrong variable
asymptotic positivity misses small parameters
source_references: developer-curated method card; named probabilistic inequalities require exact citations
known_failure_examples: many weakly likely bad events with a dense dependency graph

method_name: Encode structure in an operator and use spectral constraints
typical_inputs: a graph, incidence matrix, representation, or operator
canonical_construction: Choose an operator whose entries encode the target relation, compute trace or norm data, and convert eigenvalue constraints back into the original structure.
usual_intermediate_lemmas: inverse translation to the original object
known_failure_modes: the operator forgets orientation or multiplicity
a bound is too coarse to recover structure
nonnormal operators are treated as self-adjoint
source_references: developer-curated method card; exact spectral theorem required when used
known_failure_examples: cospectral objects with different target behavior

method_name: Translate recurrence or counting structure into a generating function
canonical_construction: Encode the exact recurrence in a formal series, manipulate it in a justified ring of series, and translate the resulting identity back coefficientwise.
known_failure_modes: analytic convergence is assumed unnecessarily or incorrectly
formal cancellation is invalid
the functional equation loses initial conditions
known_failure_examples: two sequences satisfying the same recurrence but different initial data

method_name: Choose a canonical normal form and prove confluence or uniqueness
typical_conclusions: an invariant extracted from normal form
canonical_construction: Orient relations by a well-founded complexity, prove termination and local confluence or a direct uniqueness lemma, then solve the problem on normal forms.
known_failure_modes: the reduction is not well founded
local moves are not confluent
normal form is as hard to recognize as the original property
source_references: developer-curated method card; Groebner or rewriting theorems need exact citations
known_failure_examples: two irreducible representatives of the same object

method_name: Recast the target through a dual or universal property
canonical_construction: Replace elementwise data by the representing mapping property, transport the obstruction across the duality, and prove the round-trip implication.
known_failure_modes: the duality changes variance or finiteness hypotheses
the universal property characterizes a different category
only one implication is proved
source_references: developer-curated structural card; exact category hypotheses required
known_failure_examples: double dual not recovering an infinite object

method_name: Reduce to classified irreducible endpoints and audit every exception
typical_inputs: a reduction to simple, primitive, or irreducible objects
canonical_construction: Prove the reduction independently, state the classification interface precisely, split generic and exceptional families, and assemble the endpoint checks back to the root.
known_failure_modes: the reduction assumes the classification conclusion
outer automorphisms or covering groups are omitted
small-rank coincidences are missed
diagnostic_examples: small rank and small field cases
source_references: developer-curated structural card; classification statements require exact primary citations
known_failure_examples: exceptional isomorphisms violating the generic parameter argument

method_name: Translate one bottleneck into several equivalent mathematical languages
typical_inputs: two available encodings of the same object
typical_conclusions: a proof or counterexample unavailable in the original language
canonical_construction: Write explicit dictionaries to two or more representations, prove the direction needed back to the original target, and continue only in the language that strictly reduces the decisive obligation.
known_failure_modes: the translation silently strengthens hypotheses
only notation changes
the new language loses the property being proved
diagnostic_examples: translate the smallest positive and negative examples in both directions
known_failure_examples: a reformulation whose inverse requires the root theorem
